reviewer: hand-authored (Opus) — real headless-Chrome inference smoke + driven state/matrix run + light/dark screenshots

rubric.evidence: Real end-to-end run in headless Chrome (fresh profile, served copy): Transformers.js imported, pipeline('text-generation', 'onnx-community/Falcon3-1B-Instruct', {device:'wasm', dtype:'q8'}) downloaded (~1.7 GB, onnx/model_quantized.onnx) and loaded, then generated a real streamed reply. Prompt 'Name three primary colors. Answer in one short sentence.' → 'Red, blue, and yellow are the primary colors.' 40 tokens greedy in 6187 ms (~6.5 tok/s) on the WASM/CPU backend. No 'Unsupported model type' — the llama-architecture Falcon3 export loads through the standard pipeline. Source runs this exact path in a Web Worker (models/falcon3/worker.js).
rubric.notes: Confirmed real streaming, not canned. The worker probes for a real WebGPU adapter and would use device:'webgpu' dtype:'q4f16' when present; the headless run has no GPU, so it exercised the universal WASM path and produced correct output.

rubric.evidence: Output is a coherent chat continuation conditioned on the prompt (correct factual answer). 'See inside' renders the real top-k next-token distribution (12 rows) from one forward pass over the current context, plus the exact constructed chat template. Practical page runs JSON.parse() on the model output (multilingual input → fixed English schema) to prove machine-readable shape; wild persona page streams in-character prose; multi-model page shows MiniLM cosine scores + Falcon3's grounded, cited answer.
rubric.notes: Expected shape (streamed tokens; top-k probabilities) is correct and legibly surfaced.

rubric.evidence: conformance-derived + verified in the light screenshot at-a-glance table: declares-model-id (onnx-community/Falcon3-1B-Instruct linked to card + tiiuae base), declares-task (text-generation), declares-backend ('WebAssembly (CPU) by default … auto-upgrades to WebGPU'), declares-quantisation (q8 on WASM / q4f16 on WebGPU), inference-off-main-thread (worker.js). Architecture stated honestly: 'a Llama-style decoder-only transformer (model_type: llama), 1.7B params, 131k-token vocab, 8k context'.
rubric.notes: Backend/dtype match what worker.js actually loads (adaptive device/dtype pick). No invented API surface.

rubric.evidence: Driven fresh-profile run: loader in 'download-required' state with a single labelled 'Download model (~1678 MB)' button — no silent transfer of a 1.7 GB model. Uses the shared createModelLoader auto-init architecture (shared-auto-init-loader). multi-model page shows two independent loaders (MiniLM ~30 MB + Falcon3 ~1678 MB), each honest.
rubric.notes: Absent → labelled Download; the shared loader auto-inits a cached-current copy with no button and offers a per-model clear-cache (same architecture proven on the qwen2.5-0.5b-wasm sibling). Warm-cache auto-init for this specific ~1.7 GB model was not separately driven headless (download-free harness) — openQuestion.

rubric.evidence: conformance: live-status-region (role=status aria-live present), errors-on-the-page (.err status surface + Retry via loader). Generation shows 'Generating on the WASM…' then 'Done.'; failures set status .err with the message. runs-without-webgpu-fallback: this is the universal WASM path, so it runs with no GPU by design; the worker only upgrades to WebGPU when a real adapter exists.
rubric.notes: Progress/ready/error wired accessibly on all 5 pages. Offline-cached reuse + a forced partial/evicted cache path were not separately driven this pass (openQuestions).

rubric.evidence: Main page: system-prompt textarea, message textarea, Send/Stop, example-prompt chips (incl. multilingual), sampling controls (temp/top_p ranges, max-tokens number, greedy checkbox — greedy disables temp/top_p), Clear, and the See-inside 'Show next-token distribution' button. Basics: input + Ask + chips. Practical: doc-type chips + textarea + Extract. Wild: persona chips (incl. FR/ES) + system textarea + chat. Multi-model: two loaders + KB textarea + question chips + Retrieve&answer. Driven verify: every button has an accessible name; no horizontal overflow at 1280x800 or 360x740; primary buttons min-block-size 44px, chips 40px.
rubric.notes: controls-operable is manual-evidenced. A full keyboard-only traversal (tab order + visible focus on every control across all 5 pages) is the remaining verification.

rubric.evidence: On a fresh profile the loader shows the honest absent-model 'Download' state; the transcript/answer areas show muted placeholders before a run. The visible answer in the smoke run was produced by the on-device run (streamed token-by-token). No canned result anywhere in the source.

conformance: images-have-alt (page has no content ), buttons-have-names (driven check: all buttons named across all 5 pages). Status uses role=status aria-live=polite; transcript/answer areas aria-live=polite; textareas have explicit
rubric.notes: Strong baseline. A full screen-reader pass over the streaming transcript and the top-k grid would further validate.

rubric.evidence: responsive-check: desktop clean · mobile clean. Driven matrix: scrollWidth<=innerWidth at 1280x800 AND 360x740 (DPR3, touch) on all 5 pages (main/basics/practical/wild/multi-model). Mobile screenshot read: crumbs/eyebrow/heading/lede stack, at-a-glance table and chat bubbles wrap, composer wraps, no overflow. min-inline-size:0 + overflow-wrap:anywhere on bubbles/flex items; RAG loaders use grid auto-fit minmax(min(100%,18rem),1fr) per css-layout guidance.
rubric.notes: Support recorded ok/ok on the strength of this driven pass + the real-inference smoke.

rubric.evidence: All inference (generation + top-k forward pass) runs in a Web Worker (worker.js) so the CPU-bound work never blocks the main thread; tokens stream via postMessage. Driven pages stayed responsive with zero console errors and zero failed network requests. inp-stays-low is manual-evidenced (off-thread architecture is the key mitigation).
rubric.notes: An explicit INP measurement while streaming would confirm; ~6.5 tok/s on the WASM sandbox is usable and honestly slower than a GPU path (the worker auto-upgrades to WebGPU where available).

guidanceConsulted.query: stream text token by token off main thread without INP long tasks
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Run heavy/CPU-bound work off the main thread and stream incrementally so it doesn't spike INP.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — all generation and the top-k forward pass run in models/falcon3/worker.js; tokens are posted to the UI incrementally via TextStreamer callbacks. The main thread only appends text.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: conformance inference-off-main-thread=pass; driven run stayed responsive; console clean (0 errors, 0 net failures on all 5 pages)

guidanceConsulted.query: responsive control panel flex wrap no horizontal overflow narrow mobile
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Use flex-wrap, gap, intrinsic sizing and set min-inline-size:0 on flex items with long unbreakable content; use grid auto-fit minmax(min(100%,X),1fr) for responsive card rows.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — composer/controls/chips use flex-wrap+gap; chat bubbles and composer textarea set min-inline-size:0 + overflow-wrap:anywhere; RAG loaders use grid auto-fit minmax(min(100%,18rem),1fr); glance table cells overflow-wrap:anywhere. Verified no horizontal overflow at 360x740 and 1280x800 on all 5 pages.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: responsive-check desktop+mobile clean; driven ovf(d/m)=true/true; mobile screenshot read

guidanceConsulted.query: accessible live region status announcements aria-live labelled controls
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Announce async state changes in an accessible live region; label every control via <label for>; keep buttons named and keyboard-operable; prefer disabled vs aria-disabled deliberately.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — role=status aria-live=polite loader/status regions; transcript/answer aria-live=polite; explicit <label for> on every textarea (visually-hidden where the visual label is implicit); chips grouped with aria-label; greedy checkbox toggles temp/top_p disabled state.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: conformance live-status-region=pass, buttons-have-names=pass; DOM inspection in the driven run

guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Use cross-document view transitions for catalogue↔page navigation.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied (inherited) — @view-transition navigation:auto in public/styles.css (invariant 6); all 5 pages link the shared stylesheet and crumb back to the catalogue/model page.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: shared design system; crumbs present on every page

openQuestions: Keyboard-only traversal across all 5 pages: is every control reachable in a sensible focus order with a visible focus ring, and does Enter-to-send / Shift+Enter-newline behave on a real device?
Drive the warm-cache auto-init (no button) and partial/evicted/offline states explicitly for this ~1.7 GB model (the sibling qwen2.5-0.5b-wasm proved the shared loader's warm-cache path).
On a low-memory mobile device, does the ~1.7 GB q8 download + WASM runtime stay within budget, or should the page surface a memory-pressure hint before download?
Measure Falcon3's multilingual quality across the example languages (FR/ES/PT) at 1B — where does it degrade?

followUpGoals.goal: Keyboard + screen-reader matrix pass for falcon3 across all 5 pages (focus order, visible focus, SR announcement of streamed tokens and the top-k grid).
Add a language-detection see-inside: show Falcon3's own guess of the input language and let the user compare its multilingual replies side by side.
Add a mobile memory-pressure pre-check that warns before the ~1.7 GB download on constrained devices and links smaller sibling models.

summary: falcon3 is a strong, honest, genuinely-distinct demo: TII's Falcon3-1B-Instruct (a distinct family from the Qwen/Llama/Gemma/Mistral chat models here) running on-device via Transformers.js on the universal WASM/CPU path, auto-upgrading to WebGPU when an adapter exists. Verified with a REAL headless-Chrome inference smoke (streamed 'Red, blue, and yellow are the primary colors.' at ~6.5 tok/s on WASM q8) plus a driven state/matrix pass: honest absent→Download state on a fresh profile (no auto-download of the 1.7 GB model), real top-k see-inside, clean console/network, and no horizontal overflow on desktop or mobile across all five pages (main + basics/practical multilingual-JSON/wild polyglot-persona/multi-model RAG). Light + dark both render the design system. Support recorded ok/ok. Remaining work is a keyboard/SR traversal and explicit warm/partial/offline cache-state drives. Revision 1.
